title: Terms & Conditions
content:
1. Introduction

These Terms & Conditions (“Terms”) govern access to and use of the University of Studingham Portal (“the Portal”).

By using the Portal, users acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, you must discontinue use immediately.

These Terms operate alongside the University of Studingham Privacy Policy, which explains how personal data is collected and processed.

2. Definitions

  • University means the University of Studingham.
  • Portal refers to the official online platform used for academic, administrative, and communication purposes.
  • User refers to any student, tutor, or member of administrative staff with authorised access.
  • University Account means the login credentials issued by the University to access the Portal and related services.

3. Eligibility

Access to the Portal is restricted to:

  • Enrolled students
  • Approved tutors
  • Administrative staff with authorised roles

Users must maintain an active and valid University Account to use the Portal.

The Portal is not intended for individuals under the age of 16.

4. User Responsibilities

Users agree to:

  • Provide accurate and up-to-date information
  • Keep login credentials confidential
  • Use the Portal solely for academic and university-related purposes
  • Ensure that uploaded files are safe, appropriate, and virus-free
  • Regularly check university communications for updates or instructions
  • Immediately report suspicious activity, security concerns, or account compromise

5. Acceptable Use & Code of Conduct

Users must behave respectfully and professionally while using the Portal and must not:

  • Share or allow others to use their login details
  • Attempt to access another user’s account, dashboard, or data
  • Interfere with, disrupt, or reverse-engineer any part of the Portal
  • Upload malicious software, harmful files, or unauthorised content
  • Share copyrighted course materials without permission
  • Engage in harassment, impersonation, discrimination, or abusive behaviour
  • Post inappropriate, offensive, or unlawful content in any communication areas

Violations may result in immediate account suspension or disciplinary action.

6. Academic Integrity

Users must comply with the University’s academic integrity regulations.

All coursework, files, and submissions made through the Portal must be the user’s own work unless group work is explicitly permitted. Plagiarism, cheating, or unauthorised collaboration is strictly prohibited.

7. Intellectual Property

All materials accessible through the Portal — including lecture notes, documents, videos, assessments, and digital resources — are the intellectual property of the University of Studingham or its licensors.

Users may:

  • View materials
  • Download materials
  • Use materials solely for personal academic purposes

Users may not:

  • Copy, redistribute, or publish materials
  • Share materials outside the Portal
  • Use materials for commercial or non-academic purposes

8. Portal Availability

The University aims to provide reliable access to the Portal but cannot guarantee uninterrupted service.

Temporary downtime may occur due to:

  • Scheduled maintenance
  • System updates
  • Network failures
  • Technical issues or security incidents

The University is not responsible for:

  • Data loss caused by user negligence
  • Missed deadlines resulting from personal device or internet issues
  • Incompatibility with user devices or software

In the event of a system-wide outage, the University will communicate instructions or deadline adjustments if appropriate.

9. Security Measures

The University uses a secure authentication system, encrypted password storage, and protected databases to safeguard user data.

Users must follow all security requirements, including:

  • Maintaining strong passwords
  • Updating credentials when prompted
  • Avoiding login from insecure or public devices

Suspicious activity must be reported immediately.

10. Monitoring and Logging

To maintain system security, academic integrity, and compliance:

  • User activity may be monitored
  • Interactions may be logged
  • File submissions, access history, and system usage may be recorded

Monitoring is performed in accordance with University policy and applicable laws.

11. User-Submitted Content

Users are responsible for the accuracy, legality, and safety of any content they upload.

The University is not liable for:

  • Copyright violations caused by user uploads
  • Harmful or inaccurate information uploaded by users
  • User-generated content on discussion boards, submissions, or messaging areas

The University may remove inappropriate or harmful content at its discretion.

12. Termination of Access

The University may suspend or terminate access to the Portal if:

  • A user violates these Terms
  • A user engages in harmful, disruptive, or unauthorised activities
  • A user’s enrollment, employment, or affiliation with the University ends

Termination may occur immediately if required for security purposes.

13. Limitation of Liability

The University of Studingham is not liable for:

  • Technical issues beyond its control
  • Loss of files, progress, or data due to user error
  • Device malfunction or internet failure affecting Portal use
  • Damages resulting from misuse of Portal content or functionality

Use of the Portal is at the user’s own risk.

14. Third-Party Links or Tools

If the Portal links to external websites or resources:

  • The University is not responsible for their content, security, or privacy practices
  • Users access third-party links at their own discretion

This clause ensures future compatibility even if no third-party links are currently present.

15. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by applicable University regulations and relevant regional laws.

Any disputes shall be handled according to the University’s official procedures.

16. Changes to Terms

The University reserves the right to update or amend these Terms at any time.

Users will be notified of significant changes. Continued use of the Portal after updates constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.

17. Contact Information

For questions, concerns, or support regarding these Terms, users may contact the University of Studingham through the official communication channels provided on the Portal.
